title: Law of Sines Formula
text: Recall the formula for the Law of Sines: $$\frac{a}{\sin(A)$$} $$=$$ $$\frac{b}{\sin(B)$$} $$=$$ $$\frac{c}{\sin(C)}$$.

hintAnswer: Both the angle and its opposite side must be known.
title: Known Pair
text: To set up the Law of Sines, what information must you have about at least one angle and its opposite side?

title: Required Information
text: To use the Law of Sines, you need a known "pair" (an angle and its opposite side).

title: $$\frac{AAS}{ASA}$$
text: If you know two angles and one side (AAS or ASA), can you always find a known angle-side pair?

title: Common Cases
text: Think about the standard triangle $$\frac{congruence}{similarity}$$ cases (AAS, ASA, SSA, SAS, SSS). Which ones provide a known angle-side pair?

title: SSA
text: If you know two sides and a non-included angle (SSA), can you always form a known angle-side pair?
